name: Can I install a sliding glass door myself, or do I really need a professional?
headline: Can I install a sliding glass door myself, or do I really need a professional?

mainEntity.name: Can I install a sliding glass door myself, or do I really need a professional?
mainEntity.text: Can I install a sliding glass door myself, or do I really need a professional?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you *can* install a sliding glass door yourself, but for most people it is a big, technical job that is easy to get wrong. A sliding glass door is heavy, and it has to be installed **perfectly level, plumb, and square** so it slides smoothly and seals against wind and rain. If the opening is even a little out of level, the door can stick, leak, or not lock correctly. Professionals use shims, levels, and sometimes rebuild parts of the opening to get everything lined up. You’ll need to be comfortable with: - Removing the old door and trim without damaging the structure - Checking and fixing framing and rot - Flashing and waterproofing the sill so water can’t leak into the floor - Using foam and caulk to seal gaps without warping the frame - Handling large glass panels safely Common DIY mistakes include using the wrong screws, skipping sill flashing, overfilling with spray foam (which can bow the frame), or not checking both diagonals so the frame is truly square. These errors may not show up right away, but can cause drafts, leaks, or door failure later. If you have strong carpentry skills and the right tools, DIY might save money. If not, hiring a pro is usually cheaper than fixing water damage or replacing a ruined new door later.
